enrichment.short_description: Principle is a native macOS app for designing animated, interactive prototypes with multi-screen app flows and timeline-based transitions.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Principle for Mac: design animated, interactive app prototypes with timeline transitions, multi-screen flows, and Figma/Sketch import.

features.title: Timeline-based animation
features.description: Design custom bounce, ease, and pop transitions on a timeline rather than picking from preset animations.

features.title: Multi-screen flow view
features.description: Lay out and connect entire app flows in a bird's-eye view to see how screens link together.

features.title: Figma and Sketch import
features.description: Import existing designs from Figma or Sketch with intelligent merging into Principle documents.

features.title: Device presets
features.description: Start designs with built-in presets for mobile, web, and desktop screen sizes.

features.title: Web sharing
features.description: Publish interactive prototypes to a web link that can be viewed on Windows, Linux, macOS, Android, and iOS.

features.title: Video and GIF export
features.description: Export finished animations as video files or animated GIFs for presentations and handoff.

pros: One-time license fee instead of a recurring subscription
Deep timeline control for custom animation curves, not just preset transitions
Direct import from Figma and Sketch reduces rebuild work
Prototypes can be shared via web link and viewed cross-platform
cons: macOS only — no native Windows or Linux editor
No team collaboration or commenting features mentioned on the site
$129 upfront cost with no monthly or free-forever plan

faqs.question: What platforms does Principle run on?
faqs.answer: Principle is a native macOS app (Intel and Apple Silicon); shared prototypes can be viewed on the web from Windows, Linux, macOS, Android, and iOS.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Principle can be downloaded and tried before purchasing the $129 license.

faqs.question: Can I import my existing Figma or Sketch files?
faqs.answer: Yes, Principle supports importing designs from both Figma and Sketch with intelligent merging.

faqs.question: How much does Principle cost?
faqs.answer: Principle is a one-time $129 purchase, with options to renew or add seats.

use_cases.title: Animated UI prototyping
use_cases.description: Design custom-timed transitions and micro-interactions for app screens before handing off to engineering.

use_cases.title: Multi-screen flow prototyping
use_cases.description: Map out and prototype full app flows across many connected screens in one document.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Principle?
paragraphs: Principle is a native macOS design tool for building animated, interactive prototypes of app interfaces. Instead of choosing from a list of preset transitions, designers set custom timing and easing on a timeline for each interaction.
The tool also supports laying out entire multi-screen app flows in a single bird's-eye view, and can import existing designs directly from Figma or Sketch.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Principle is for
paragraphs: Principle is aimed at product and UX designers on macOS who need finer control over animation timing than static design tools provide, and who want to share working prototypes with teammates via a web link.

enrichment.short_description: Sintra AI offers 12 pre-built AI agents that automate tasks like social media, copywriting, data analysis, and customer support.
enrichment.starting_price: $48.50/month (monthly billing; lower per-month rates on longer commitments)
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Sintra AI: 12 role-based AI agents that automate social media, copywriting, support, and other business tasks.

features.title: 12 specialized AI agents
features.description: Pre-trained AI employees for roles such as social media manager, copywriter, data analyst, and customer support.

features.title: 50+ tool integrations
features.description: Connects to Gmail, Google Calendar, Outlook, Facebook, Instagram, Notion, Slack, and other business tools.

features.title: Brain AI context memory
features.description: Learns business-specific context so agents can act with awareness of the company's data and history.

features.title: Multiple business profiles
features.description: Supports up to 5 customizable business profiles per account for managing different brands or clients.

features.title: Team workspace
features.description: Provides a shared collaboration workspace for teams to manage AI agents together.

features.title: 24/7 task automation
features.description: Agents run continuously to execute tasks like email handling and scheduling without manual triggers.

pros: Covers multiple business functions (social, copy, support, data) from one platform
Broad integration list including Gmail, Slack, and Notion
14-day money-back guarantee
Multi-business-profile support for agencies or multi-brand teams
cons: No free trial — paid subscription starts immediately
Lowest advertised per-month price requires a 12-month prepaid commitment
No on-premise or self-hosted option mentioned

faqs.question: Does Sintra AI offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: No free trial is advertised; Sintra offers a 14-day money-back guarantee instead.

faqs.question: How many AI agents does Sintra AI include?
faqs.answer: The platform includes 12 pre-built AI employees for different business roles.

faqs.question: What does Sintra AI integrate with?
faqs.answer: It integrates with 50+ tools including Gmail, Google Calendar, Outlook, Facebook, Instagram, Notion, and Slack.

faqs.question: How much does Sintra AI cost?
faqs.answer: Monthly billing starts at $48.50/month, with lower per-month rates available on quarterly ($23.60/mo) and annual ($15.60/mo) plans.

use_cases.title: Automating routine marketing tasks
use_cases.description: Delegate social media posting and copywriting to dedicated AI agents.

use_cases.title: Customer support coverage
use_cases.description: Use an AI support agent to handle customer inquiries around the clock.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Sintra AI?
paragraphs: Sintra AI is a platform of 12 pre-built, role-based AI agents — described as "AI employees" — that handle tasks such as social media management, copywriting, data analysis, and customer support.
Agents connect to over 50 business tools, including Gmail, Slack, and Notion, and use a context-learning system the company calls Brain AI to act with awareness of a business's own data.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Sintra AI is for
paragraphs: Sintra AI targets small business owners, solo founders, and small teams who want to offload recurring marketing, support, and admin tasks to automated agents rather than hiring for each role.

enrichment.short_description: Relevance AI is a platform for building autonomous AI agents that handle sales, support, marketing, and operations workflows.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Relevance AI: build and deploy autonomous AI agents for sales, support, marketing, and ops with 1,000+ integrations.

features.title: No-code agent builder
features.description: Build autonomous AI agents without writing code, using pre-built templates for common workflows.

features.title: Multi-LLM support
features.description: Agents can run on multiple models (Claude, GPT, Gemini, and others) with automatic model selection for cost control.

features.title: 1,000+ app integrations
features.description: Connects to tools like Salesforce, HubSpot, LinkedIn, Slack, and Gmail.

features.title: Human-in-the-loop approvals
features.description: Agents can pause for human approval before completing sensitive actions.

features.title: Enterprise security controls
features.description: SOC 2 and GDPR compliance, SSO/SAML, audit logs, and data residency options.

features.title: Real-time cost monitoring
features.description: Tracks task-level cost and usage in real time.

pros: Very large integration library (1,000+ apps advertised)
Support for multiple LLM providers with automatic cost optimization
Enterprise-grade security options (SSO, SAML, audit logs, SOC 2)
Pre-built templates speed up building common agent workflows
cons: Detailed self-serve pricing tiers are not published — the public pricing page shows only a custom Enterprise plan
Usage-based/per-task pricing can make monthly costs harder to predict than a flat subscription

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for Relevance AI?
faqs.answer: The site offers a "Try it out" option, though detailed self-serve plan pricing isn't published.

faqs.question: How is Relevance AI priced?
faqs.answer: Pricing is usage-based and billed per task, with examples cited as low as $0.02 per simple task; the public pricing page otherwise shows a custom Enterprise plan.

faqs.question: What integrations does Relevance AI support?
faqs.answer: It advertises 1,000+ integrations, including Salesforce, HubSpot, LinkedIn, Slack, and Gmail.

faqs.question: Does Relevance AI support multiple AI models?
faqs.answer: Yes, agents can use Claude, GPT, Gemini, and other models with automatic optimization.

use_cases.title: Sales workflow automation
use_cases.description: Automate lead enrichment, meeting scheduling, and proposal generation.

use_cases.title: Customer success operations
use_cases.description: Deploy agents for deal review and customer follow-up tasks.

use_cases.title: Enterprise process automation
use_cases.description: Build agents across HR, marketing, and operations workflows with governance controls.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Relevance AI?
paragraphs: Relevance AI is a platform for building and deploying autonomous AI agents that carry out specific business tasks, such as lead enrichment, meeting scheduling, or proposal generation.
Agents can run on multiple LLM providers, connect to over 1,000 third-party apps, and include human-in-the-loop approval steps and enterprise security controls.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Relevance AI is for
paragraphs: Relevance AI is aimed at teams — particularly in sales, customer success, marketing, and operations — that want to automate repetitive workflows with AI agents rather than building custom automation in-house.

enrichment.short_description: Lindy is an AI work assistant that manages email, scheduling, meeting notes, and CRM follow-ups across connected apps.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Lindy: an AI assistant for inbox triage, meeting prep, scheduling, and CRM follow-ups, with a 7-day free trial.

features.title: Email management
features.description: Prioritizes inbox items and drafts replies matched to the user's writing voice.

features.title: Meeting preparation and notes
features.description: Prepares briefs before meetings and records notes with action items afterward.

features.title: Calendar coordination
features.description: Schedules and coordinates meetings across calendars.

features.title: Automated follow-ups
features.description: Sends post-meeting follow-up messages automatically.

features.title: CRM integration
features.description: Updates records in HubSpot and Salesforce.

features.title: Mobile delegation
features.description: Tasks can be delegated via iMessage or SMS in addition to the web app.

pros: 7-day free trial before committing to a paid plan
Delegate tasks via text message, not just a web dashboard
Integrates directly with major CRMs (HubSpot, Salesforce)
Multiple usage tiers scale connected inboxes and features
cons: No free-forever plan — only a 7-day trial
Higher-usage features like computer use are gated to the Pro tier and above
Enterprise features (SSO, SCIM, audit logs) require a custom-priced plan

faqs.question: Does Lindy offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 7-day free trial is available before paid billing starts.

faqs.question: How much does Lindy cost?
faqs.answer: Plans start at $49.99/month (Plus), with Pro at $99.99/month and Max at $199.99/month; Enterprise is custom-priced.

faqs.question: What apps does Lindy integrate with?
faqs.answer: Gmail, Outlook, Google Calendar, Slack, Notion, HubSpot, Salesforce, Microsoft Teams, and Zoom.

faqs.question: Can I delegate tasks to Lindy from my phone?
faqs.answer: Yes, tasks can be delegated via iMessage or SMS.

use_cases.title: Inbox and calendar management
use_cases.description: Automate email triage, drafting, and meeting scheduling.

use_cases.title: Meeting operations
use_cases.description: Get meeting prep briefs, notes, and follow-up emails handled automatically.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Lindy?
paragraphs: Lindy is an AI work assistant that handles administrative work across a user's connected apps — triaging and drafting email, preparing for and taking notes on meetings, coordinating calendars, and updating CRM records.
Work can be delegated through a web interface, inside integrated apps, or by texting Lindy directly via iMessage or SMS.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Lindy is for
paragraphs: Lindy is built for professionals who want an AI assistant to handle recurring inbox, scheduling, and follow-up work, with plans that scale from individual use up to enterprise teams needing SSO and audit logs.

enrichment.short_description: Dust is a platform for building custom AI agents that connect to company knowledge like Slack, Notion, and Drive to take action.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Dust: build custom AI agents that read your company's Slack, Notion, and Drive data and take action via built-in and custom tools.

features.title: Agent builder
features.description: Create custom AI agents without engineering support, using built-in and custom tools.

features.title: Knowledge connectors
features.description: Agents can read company data from sources like Slack, Notion, and Google Drive.

features.title: Multi-model support
features.description: Agents can run on leading models such as GPT and Claude.

features.title: MCP tool support
features.description: Agents can connect to external tools via the Model Context Protocol.

features.title: Native integrations
features.description: Includes integrations such as Zendesk, Slack, and a Chrome extension.

features.title: Enterprise controls
features.description: Supports SSO, SCIM, SOC 2, and an optional zero data retention mode.

pros: Agent builder is accessible to non-technical team members
Connects directly to common company knowledge sources (Slack, Notion, Drive)
Supports multiple leading LLMs rather than locking into one model
Enterprise security options including SSO, SCIM, and SOC 2
cons: Detailed self-serve pricing tiers could not be independently confirmed from the official site at time of writing
Some enterprise controls (SCIM, single-tenant deployment) are limited to higher/custom tiers

faqs.question: What can Dust agents connect to?
faqs.answer: Dust agents can read company knowledge from sources like Slack, Notion, and Google Drive, and take action through built-in and custom tools.

faqs.question: Which AI models does Dust support?
faqs.answer: Dust supports multiple leading models, including GPT and Claude.

faqs.question: Does Dust support the Model Context Protocol (MCP)?
faqs.answer: Yes, agents can use MCP to connect to external tools.

faqs.question: What enterprise security features does Dust offer?
faqs.answer: SSO, SCIM, SOC 2 compliance, and an optional zero data retention mode.

use_cases.title: Internal knowledge assistants
use_cases.description: Build agents that answer questions using company docs stored in Slack, Notion, and Drive.

use_cases.title: Cross-department automation
use_cases.description: Deploy agents for sales, support, and marketing workflows without engineering involvement.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Dust?
paragraphs: Dust is a platform for building custom AI agents that read a company's own knowledge — from sources like Slack, Notion, and Google Drive — and take action using built-in and custom tools.
Agents can run on multiple leading models and connect to external tools through the Model Context Protocol (MCP), with enterprise controls like SSO, SCIM, and SOC 2 available for larger teams.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Dust is for
paragraphs: Dust targets companies that want non-technical teams to build AI agents grounded in their own internal data, rather than relying solely on engineering to wire up custom automation.

enrichment.short_description: Stack AI is a no-code platform for building and deploying enterprise AI agents and workflows.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Stack AI: build no-code AI agent workflows for finance, legal, healthcare, and support with 100+ integrations.

features.title: No-code workflow builder
features.description: Drag-and-drop interface for assembling AI agent workflows without writing code.

features.title: Multi-model support
features.description: LLM-agnostic platform that works with multiple AI models.

features.title: 100+ integrations
features.description: Connects to existing business systems.

features.title: Industry-specific templates
features.description: Pre-built workflows for finance, legal, healthcare, customer service, and IT.

features.title: Governance controls
features.description: Audit logs and human-in-the-loop review built into workflows.

features.title: Flexible deployment
features.description: Available as multi-tenant cloud, VPC, or on-premise installation.

pros: Free plan available with 500 runs/month
No-code builder makes agent creation accessible without engineering
Deployment flexibility (cloud, VPC, on-prem)
Broad security certifications: SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA, ISO 27001, GDPR
cons: No mid-tier paid plan is publicly listed — pricing jumps from a 500-run Free plan straight to custom Enterprise
Free plan is limited to 2 projects and 1 seat

faqs.question: Is there a free plan for Stack AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, a Free plan includes 500 runs/month, 2 projects, and 1 seat.

faqs.question: How is Stack AI priced above the free tier?
faqs.answer: Beyond the Free plan, pricing is custom (Enterprise), available on request.

faqs.question: What compliance certifications does Stack AI have?
faqs.answer: SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA, GDPR, and ISO 27001.

faqs.question: Can Stack AI be deployed on-premise?
faqs.answer: Yes, in addition to multi-tenant cloud, VPC and on-premise deployment options are available.

use_cases.title: Document and contract review
use_cases.description: Automate contract review and claims processing with no-code agent workflows.

use_cases.title: Support ticket triage
use_cases.description: Route and triage customer support tickets using AI agents.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Stack AI?
paragraphs: Stack AI is a no-code platform for building enterprise AI agent workflows using a drag-and-drop interface. It's LLM-agnostic and includes over 100 integrations with existing business systems.
The platform ships with industry-specific templates for finance, legal, healthcare, customer service, and IT, plus governance features like audit logs and human-in-the-loop review.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Stack AI is for
paragraphs: Stack AI is aimed at enterprises that need to deploy AI agents for regulated or process-heavy work — such as contract review or claims processing — with deployment options including on-premise and VPC installs.

enrichment.short_description: Svix is a webhooks-as-a-service platform that sends and manages webhooks with automatic retries, monitoring, and security hardening.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Svix: webhooks-as-a-service with automatic retries, monitoring, and a 50,000 message/month free tier.

features.title: Automatic retries
features.description: Retries failed webhook deliveries automatically.

features.title: Monitoring and logs
features.description: Dashboards for tracking delivery status and debugging.

features.title: Security hardening
features.description: Protects against SSRF and replay attacks.

features.title: Embeddable customer portal
features.description: Lets your customers manage their own webhook subscriptions inside your app.

features.title: Standard Webhooks compliance
features.description: Follows the Standard Webhooks specification.

features.title: Broad SDK support
features.description: Official SDKs for JavaScript/TypeScript, Python, Go, Kotlin, Ruby, C#, Java, PHP, and Rust.

pros: Free tier includes 50,000 messages/month with no credit card required
Wide language/SDK coverage
SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA, and PCI-DSS compliance
Embeddable portal lets end customers self-manage webhook settings
cons: Professional plan starts at $490/month — a large step up from the free tier
Enterprise pricing is quote-only

faqs.question: Is Svix free to start?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan includes 50,000 messages/month with no credit card required.

faqs.question: How much does Svix's paid plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Professional plan starts at $490/month for 50,000 messages/month with a higher SLA and throughput.

faqs.question: What compliance certifications does Svix have?
faqs.answer: SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, GDPR, and CCPA.

faqs.question: What languages does Svix provide SDKs for?
faqs.answer: JavaScript/TypeScript, Python, Go, Kotlin, Ruby, C#, Java, PHP, and Rust.

use_cases.title: Outbound webhook delivery for SaaS products
use_cases.description: Give customers a reliable, self-service way to receive event notifications from your API.

use_cases.title: Customer-facing webhook management
use_cases.description: Embed a webhook management portal directly into your product for end users.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Svix?
paragraphs: Svix is a webhooks-as-a-service platform that handles sending, receiving, and managing webhooks, including automatic retries, monitoring, security hardening, and idempotency.
It also provides an embeddable customer-facing portal so a company's own customers can manage their webhook subscriptions.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Svix is for
paragraphs: Svix is built for engineering teams at SaaS and API companies that need to deliver reliable outbound webhooks to customers without building retry, security, and monitoring infrastructure in-house.

enrichment.short_description: Hookdeck is an event gateway for receiving, transforming, routing, and monitoring webhooks, plus outbound delivery via Outpost.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Hookdeck: manage inbound and outbound webhooks with routing, transformation, and full observability, free up to 10,000 events/month.

features.title: Event Gateway
features.description: Receives, transforms, and routes inbound webhooks with full observability.

features.title: Outpost
features.description: Open-source outbound webhook delivery with multi-destination support.

features.title: CLI
features.description: Debug webhooks locally on localhost.

features.title: Console
features.description: Inspect webhook payloads without needing an account.

features.title: Radar
features.description: Alerts on third-party webhook latency issues.

features.title: MCP server
features.description: Integrates with AI coding tools such as Cursor and Claude.

pros: Free Developer tier includes 10,000 events/month
Outpost outbound component is open source
MCP server support for AI coding assistants
Enterprise security options: SOC 2 Type II, SSO, SCIM, RBAC, MFA
cons: Growth plan (uptime guarantees, SSO) starts at $499/month
Additional events and throughput beyond plan limits are billed separately
Enterprise pricing is quote-only

faqs.question: Is Hookdeck free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Developer plan is free forever with 10,000 events/month.

faqs.question: How much do paid Hookdeck plans cost?
faqs.answer: Team starts at $39/month and Growth starts at $499/month; Enterprise is custom.

faqs.question: What is Outpost?
faqs.answer: Outpost is Hookdeck's open-source product for outbound webhook delivery to customers.

faqs.question: Does Hookdeck integrate with AI coding tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, it provides an MCP server for tools like Cursor and Claude.

use_cases.title: Inbound webhook management
use_cases.description: Receive, transform, and route incoming webhooks with full observability.

use_cases.title: Outbound webhook delivery for SaaS
use_cases.description: Send reliable outbound webhooks to customers using the open-source Outpost component.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Hookdeck?
paragraphs: Hookdeck provides infrastructure for managing webhooks across their full lifecycle. Its Event Gateway receives, transforms, and routes inbound webhooks with full observability, while Outpost handles outbound webhook delivery for APIs and SaaS platforms.
It also includes a local-debugging CLI, a no-account payload inspector (Console), and third-party latency alerting (Radar).

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Hookdeck is for
paragraphs: Hookdeck is aimed at backend and platform engineering teams that need reliable inbound and outbound webhook infrastructure without building retry, routing, and observability tooling themselves.

enrichment.short_description: Trigger.dev is a TypeScript platform for running long-running background jobs and AI agent workflows with retries, queues, and observability.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Trigger.dev: build durable background jobs and AI agent workflows in TypeScript, with a free tier and usage-based pricing.

features.title: AI agent tasks
features.description: Build tool-calling AI agents with human-in-the-loop approval steps.

features.title: Realtime updates
features.description: Stream LLM responses and task status to frontends.

features.title: Durable cron scheduling
features.description: Run scheduled jobs without timeout limitations.

features.title: Concurrency and queues
features.description: Control how many tasks run at once.

features.title: Observability
features.description: Real-time monitoring, logging, and tracing with advanced filtering.

features.title: Automatic retries
features.description: Configurable retry logic with exponential backoff.

features.title: Build extensions
features.description: Add support for Python, Prisma, Puppeteer, FFmpeg, and esbuild.

pros: Free tier with monthly usage credits to start building
Usage-based compute pricing — pay only while code executes
Works across many frameworks (Next.js, Remix, Bun, SvelteKit, Astro, and more)
Extension system adds Python, Puppeteer, and FFmpeg support to a TypeScript-first platform
cons: Additional concurrency, team seats, and Realtime connections beyond plan limits cost extra
Enterprise plan is custom-priced

faqs.question: Does Trigger.dev have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan includes $5/month in credits, 20 concurrent runs, and unlimited tasks.

faqs.question: How is Trigger.dev priced?
faqs.answer: Plans start at $10/month (Hobby) and $50/month (Pro), plus usage-based compute charged per second of execution.

faqs.question: What languages does Trigger.dev support?
faqs.answer: It's built for TypeScript/JavaScript, with Python support available via a build extension.

faqs.question: Can Trigger.dev build AI agents?
faqs.answer: Yes, it supports tool-calling AI agents with human-in-the-loop approval and realtime streaming of responses.

use_cases.title: Background job processing
use_cases.description: Run long-running or scheduled tasks without managing servers or worrying about timeouts.

use_cases.title: AI agent workflows
use_cases.description: Build agents that call tools and stream results to a frontend in real time.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Trigger.dev?
paragraphs: Trigger.dev is a platform for building and deploying background tasks and AI agent workflows in TypeScript, with built-in retries, queuing, observability, and elastic scaling.
It's designed to remove the need to manage servers or work around request timeouts for long-running jobs.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Trigger.dev is for
paragraphs: Trigger.dev is aimed at developers building on TypeScript/JavaScript frameworks who need durable background jobs or AI agent workflows without standing up their own queue and worker infrastructure.

enrichment.short_description: Encore lets developers declare backend infrastructure in TypeScript or Go code and auto-provisions it to their own AWS or GCP account.
enrichment.starting_price: From $49/member/month (Pro plan; Free plan also available)
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Encore: define backend infrastructure in TypeScript or Go and deploy it automatically to your own AWS or GCP account.

features.title: Infrastructure as code
features.description: Declare databases, Pub/Sub topics, and storage buckets directly in application code.

features.title: Local development environment
features.description: Run the whole backend locally with one command, mirroring production.

features.title: Preview environments
features.description: Get a full production-like environment for every pull request.

features.title: Built-in observability
features.description: Automatic distributed tracing, metrics, and architecture diagrams.

features.title: Service Catalog
features.description: Auto-generated API documentation and service explorer.

features.title: MCP server
features.description: Provides context to AI coding tools for guardrailed code generation.

pros: Free forever tier for local development
Deploys directly into your own AWS or GCP account — no infrastructure vendor lock-in
Automatic tracing, metrics, and architecture diagrams with no extra setup
200+ engineering teams and 12,000+ GitHub stars (per site)
cons: Pro plan combines per-member, per-environment, and per-resource charges, which can add up
Only TypeScript and Go are supported languages

faqs.question: Is Encore free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, there's a free-forever tier for local development with 2 free cloud dev environments.

faqs.question: How much does Encore's Pro plan cost?
faqs.answer: From $49/member/month, plus $99/month per AWS/GCP environment and $2.50/month per resource.

faqs.question: Which languages does Encore support?
faqs.answer: TypeScript and Go.

faqs.question: Where does Encore deploy my backend?
faqs.answer: To your own AWS or GCP account, rather than a proprietary Encore-hosted cloud.

use_cases.title: Cloud backend development
use_cases.description: Build and deploy backend services without hand-writing Terraform or cloud config.

use_cases.title: Preview environments for PRs
use_cases.description: Spin up a full production-like environment automatically for every pull request.

features: Deploy to your own AWS & GCP

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Encore?
paragraphs: Encore is a backend platform that lets developers define infrastructure — databases, Pub/Sub topics, storage buckets, and more — directly in TypeScript or Go application code, instead of separate config like Terraform.

It automatically provisions and deploys that infrastructure to the user's own AWS or GCP account, and includes built-in distributed tracing, a local dev environment that mirrors production, and preview environments for every pull request.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Encore is for
paragraphs: Encore is aimed at backend engineering teams building on AWS or GCP with TypeScript or Go who want infrastructure defined in code and automatic observability, without manually maintaining separate IaC tooling.
